compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Context

The three example skills (/rebar-brainstorm, /rebar-debug, /rebar-implement) each carry their own inline TDD prose. rebar-debug (Steps 4-5) and rebar-implement (Phase 4) are strong on RED->GREEN integrity (RED-first, held-out oracle, mutation check, refactoring litmus, no test-weakening) but under-specified on choosing the RIGHT test: no existing-coverage inventory before authoring, no test-tier selection rule, no precondition/collateral-invariant oracle guidance, no fault-realism rules, no proof the test gates CI. rebar-brainstorm treats testing only via the plan-review gate docs. An external assessment report proposed improvements; this epic adopts the findings judged valid, scaled to the skills' 'scale ceremony to the bug' ethos. Plan-review E5 is deliberately left untouched: it already covers change-detector, proxy-validation (the tier rule at plan level), and missing failure paths, with tuned anti-over-fire guards.

## Decomposition (two child tasks, one Gerrit change each)

- Task 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900 (witchy-sphingine-topi): create the shared test-design standard at examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md (canonical file + byte-identical per-skill copies, drift-gated by a unit test) and integrate it into the three SKILL.md files, rebar-debug's llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md (its Phase 2 RED-artifact wording defers to the quantified eval standard), and the examples README.
- Task e8d3-03b6-cf2d-46ae (deserving-cloistered-tayra): add the two uncovered antipatterns (non-gating test; unrealistic fault injection / sleep-only race sync) with false-positive guards to src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-tests.md, AND extend src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml with recall + no-fire cases for both, so the eval suite owns the new rubric surface.

## Success Criteria

- One shared test-design reference exists under examples/claude-skills/shared/ and is loaded by all three skills at the point each incorporates testing.
- The skills' held-out discipline is preserved (the fix/implementation subagent still never sees held-out test code).
- The code-review tests criterion gains only the two uncovered antipatterns, each with explicit false-positive guards, with no posture/routing change, and each new antipattern has eval recall + no-fire coverage.
- CI gates (lint, prompt-index drift, criteria-routing parity) stay green.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The Gerrit change adding examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md and the three SKILL.md integrations is Verified +1 and merged (task 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900 closed).
- [ ] The Gerrit change updating src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-tests.md and code-review-tests.eval.yaml is Verified +1 and merged (task e8d3-03b6-cf2d-46ae closed).
- [ ] git diff of the landed changes shows no edit to src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_E5.md, either criteria_routing.json, or any threshold/posture field.
- [ ] Held-out discipline preserved, proven mechanically: after the skill edits, `grep -c 'held-out\|held out' examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md` shows the hold-out language still present in both, the rebar-debug Step 5 fixer handoff enumerates only root cause, evidence, reproduction steps, and the contract card (no test path), and the diff adds no instruction to show held-out test code to a fix/implementation subagent.
compiled_state.title: Shared TDD test-design standard for the example skills + code-review tests-criterion gap fix
